title: Rich Chewy Irish Brown Sugar Cookies
description: A decadent, chewy Irish-inspired cookie featuring dark brown sugar, coconut oil, almond flour, and a hint of molasses-paleo-friendly and perfect for midnight cravings.

ingredients.name: Almond flour
ingredients.notes: Finely ground for a smooth texture

ingredients.name: Coconut flour
ingredients.notes: Adds structure and a slightly sweet, nutty flavor

ingredients.name: Dark brown sugar
ingredients.notes: Use 80% molasses for deeper color and taste

ingredients.name: Molasses
ingredients.notes: Adds moisture and deep caramel flavor

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, whisk together the almond flour, coconut flour, baking soda, sea salt, and cinnamon until well combined.
In a separate bowl, beat the egg, melted coconut oil, dark brown sugar, molasses, and vanilla extract until smooth.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until a sticky dough forms. If the dough feels too wet, add a tablespoon of almond flour to firm it up.
Using a tablespoon, scoop dough onto the prepared baking sheet, spacing cookies 2 inches apart.
Press each cookie lightly with the back of a spoon to create a slightly flattened shape.
Bake for 12-15 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the centers still feel soft.
Remove from oven and let cookies cool on the sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
Enjoy the cookies warm or store in an airtight container for up to a week. They become even chewier as they cool.
Optional: Sprinkle a pinch of sea salt on top before baking for a sweet-salty finish.
